name: mentha piperita
mechanism: Menthol-rich essential oils show antispasmodic and sensory receptor activity consistent with digestive and cooling effects in studied preparations.
safetyNotes: Use caution in reflux-sensitive individuals and with concentrated essential oil products.
cooling. Herbs often act via neurotransmitter modulation
nan.. Herbs often act via neurotransmitter modulation
description: A hybrid mint (Mentha aquatica × Mentha spicata) widely used for digestive support, flavoring, and aromatherapy; possesses antispasmodic, carminative, and cooling properties.
legalStatus: Unregulated; approved in supplements and food
preparation: Leaves dried or fresh for teas, tinctures, and essential oil distillation; widely used for digestion and headaches
region: Widely cultivated and used globally in culinary, medicinal, and aromatic preparations.
summary: Mentha piperita has human and preclinical evidence for gastrointestinal symptom support, while efficacy depends on preparation and indication.
